Form S-3 — Shelf Registration Statement

Issuer: Acme Therapeutics, Inc. (CIK 0001234567)
Accession Number: 0001234567-25-000042
Filed: 2026-03-15

PROSPECTUS SUMMARY

We may offer and sell from time to time, in one or more offerings, up to
$300 million in aggregate offering amount of our common stock, preferred
stock, debt securities, warrants, and units. We refer to our common
stock, preferred stock, debt securities, warrants, and units collectively
as the "securities." This prospectus describes some of the general terms
that may apply to these securities and the general manner in which they
may be offered. The specific terms of any securities to be offered, and
the specific manner in which they may be offered, will be described in
one or more supplements to this prospectus.

USE OF PROCEEDS

Unless we specify otherwise in the applicable prospectus supplement, we
intend to use the net proceeds from the sale of the securities for
general corporate purposes, including working capital, research and
development activities, capital expenditures, and to fund the Phase II
clinical trial of our lead therapeutic candidate ACM-101 in patients
with relapsed multiple myeloma.

PLAN OF DISTRIBUTION

We may offer the securities directly, through agents, or through
underwriters or dealers, including in at-the-market offerings as defined
in Rule 415(a)(4) under the Securities Act, on the Nasdaq Global Select
Market or other exchanges. Any underwriters, dealers, or agents will be
identified, and any compensation they receive will be described, in the
applicable prospectus supplement.

The securities may be sold for cash or other consideration. The
securities may be sold at fixed prices, at market prices prevailing at
the time of sale, at prices related to such prevailing market prices, or
at negotiated prices.

DILUTION

The sale of the securities offered hereby will result in dilution of the
ownership interest of our existing stockholders. To the extent that we
issue and sell additional shares of common stock or securities
convertible into or exercisable for shares of common stock, including
through registered direct offerings or at-the-market offerings, our
existing stockholders may experience substantial dilution. The exact
amount of dilution will depend on the number of shares sold and the
price at which they are sold.

RISK FACTORS

Investing in our securities involves a high degree of risk. You should
carefully consider the risks and uncertainties described under the
heading "Risk Factors" contained in our most recent Annual Report on
Form 10-K, as updated by our subsequent Quarterly Reports on Form 10-Q,
which are incorporated by reference herein.
